mechanism_narrative: A rising level of real retail sales (RSAFS) signals strengthening consumer demand, which drives higher corporate revenues and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month RSAFS increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window, or rank correlation between RSAFS change and returns falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5 percent, elevated labor slack suppresses wage income and consumer spending, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is above 8.5%, or the mean return conditional on this trigger is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in industrial production and payroll employment indicate a synchronized contraction across supply and labor markets, amplifying recession risk and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal does not produce negative average 20-day returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) above individual series in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days exclusively during early recovery periods when capacity utilization is rebounding from a deep trough.
failure_condition: In post-recession quarters, positive INDPRO changes fail to correlate with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns (rank IC < 0.05), or the strategy Sharpe ratio conditional on this regime is not higher than in non-recovery regimes (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25 percent signals the zero lower bound, where liquidity provisions and forward guidance suppress discount rates and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns conditional on FEDFUNDS below 0.25% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or positive return frequency falls below 52% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 600 thousand units indicate depressed construction activity and weak residential investment, reducing aggregate demand and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 600k, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations, or the average conditional return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals aggregate demand expansion and improving corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth does not exhibit a positive rank correlation (IC < 0.03) with subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year consumer price inflation exceeding 3.0 percent erodes real corporate margins and triggers expectations of monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year exceeds 3%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.
